title: Uragoga suaveolens
taxonomy: Uragoga suaveolens belongs to the family Rubiaceae. It is a flowering plant native to tropical Africa. The classification of Uragoga suaveolens is further supported by its placement in the genus Uragoga, which is a part of the tribe Psychotrieae. The plant's classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other species. More research is needed to confirm the exact classification and relationships within the plant's family and genus.
uses: Uragoga suaveolens is known to have various medicinal properties and uses in traditional medicine. It is traditionally used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal uses and effects.
